title: Response Envelope
description: The canonical shape every Foundation (and future Phase 2A) backend function returns to its caller. Every response is exactly this shape whether the operation succeeded or failed — a caller never receives a raw exception or an ad hoc error object. See response-envelope.md for rationale and usage.

status.description: Discriminates the envelope. "ok" means data is the result payload and error is null. "error" means data is null and error describes what went wrong.

data.description: The operation's result payload when status is "ok". Always null when status is "error".

code.description: A stable, machine-readable error identifier (e.g. "FOUNDATION_UNEXPECTED_ERROR", "FOUNDATION_NOT_FOUND"). Callers may branch on this; it must never change meaning once shipped — add a new code instead of repurposing one.

message.description: A human-readable, non-technical message safe to show a user. Never a stack trace, a raw exception message, or implementation detail (docs/04 Error State: friendly, actionable, never technical).
